contentBlocks.original:
स॒हस्र॑शीर्​षा॒ पुरु॑षः । स॒ह॒स्रा॒क्ष-स्स॒हस्र॑पात् ।
स भूमिं॑-विँ॒श्वतो॑ वृ॒त्वा । अत्य॑तिष्ठद्दशाङ्गु॒लम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa̠hasra̍śīr​ṣā̠ puru̍ṣaḥ । sa̠ha̠srā̠kṣa-ssa̠hasra̍pāt ।
sa bhūmi̍ṃ vi̠śvatō̍ vṛ̠tvā । atya̍tiṣṭhaddaśāṅgu̠lam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The purusha (universal being) has thousand heads, thousand eyes and thousand feet. Thousand signifies innumerable which points to the omnipresence of the universal being. He envelops the world from all sides i.e. he pervades each part of the creation, and extends beyond in the ten directions represented by ten fingers.
contentBlocks.meanings: सहस्रशीर्षा (sahasraśīrṣā) – thousand-headed (sahasra:thousand | śīrṣā:headed); पुरुषः (puruṣaḥ) – universal being; सहस्राक्षः (sahasrākṣaḥ) – thousand-eyed (sahasra:thousand | akṣaḥ:eyed); सहस्रपात् (sahasrapāt) – thousand-footed (sahasra:thousand | pāt:footed); स (sa) – he; भूमिं (bhūmiṃ) – earth; विश्वतः (viśvataḥ) – from all sides; वृत्वा (vṛtvā) – enveloping; अत्यतिष्ठत् (atyatiṣṭhat) – extended beyond; दशाङुलम् (daśāṅulam) – ten fingers (daśa:ten | aṅulam:fingers);

contentBlocks.original:
पुरु॑ष ए॒वेदग्ं सर्वम्᳚ । यद्भू॒तं-यँच्च॒ भव्यम्᳚ ।
उ॒तामृ॑त॒त्व स्येशा॑नः । यदन्ने॑नाति॒रोह॑ति ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puru̍ṣa ē̠vēdagṃ sarvam̎ । yadbhū̠taṃ yachcha̠ bhavyam̎ ।
u̠tāmṛ̍ta̠tva syēśā̍naḥ । yadannē̍nāti̠rōha̍ti ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The purusha is indeed all this (creation) in essence; that which existed in the past, and that which will exist in the future, everything i.e the whole creation is woven by the immortal essence of the great lord (purusha); by becoming food of which (i.e. by getting consumed in whose immortal essence through surrender) one transcends the gross world and becomes immortal.
contentBlocks.meanings: पुरुष (puruṣa) – the purusha; एव (eva) – indeed; इदं (idaṃ) – this; सर्वं (sarvaṃ) – all; यत् (yat) – which; भूतं (bhūtaṃ) – existed in the past; यत् (yat) – and which; च (ca) – and; भव्यम् (bhavyam) – will exist in the future; उत (uta) – also; अमृतत्वस्य (amṛtatvasya) – of immortality; ईशानः (īśānaḥ) – lord; यत् (yat) – which; अन्नेन (annena) – by food; अतिरोहति (atirohati) – transcends;

contentBlocks.original:
ए॒तावा॑नस्य महि॒मा । अतो॒ ज्यायाग्॑श्च॒ पूरु॑षः ।
पादो᳚-ऽस्य॒ विश्वा॑ भू॒तानि॑ । त्रि॒पाद॑स्या॒मृत॑-न्दि॒वि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ē̠tāvā̍nasya mahi̠mā । atō̠ jyāyāg̍ścha̠ pūru̍ṣaḥ ।
pādō̎-'sya̠ viśvā̍ bhū̠tāni̍ । tri̠pāda̍syā̠mṛta̍-ndi̠vi ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The purusha is greater than all the greatness which can be expressed by words. His one part has become all these (visible) worlds, and his three parts rest in the immortal world of the transcendence.
contentBlocks.meanings: एतावान् (etāvān) – this much; अस्य (asya) – of him; महिमा (mahimā) – greatness; अतः (ataḥ) – than this; ज्यायान् (jyāyān) – greater; च (ca) – and; पूरुषः (pūruṣaḥ) – the purusha; पादः (pādaḥ) – one part; अस्य (asya) – of him; विश्वा (viśvā) – all; भूतानि (bhūtāni) – beings; त्रिपादस्य (tripādasya) – of his three parts (tri:three | pādasya:of the parts); अमृतं (amṛtam) – immortal; दिवि (divi) – in the heaven;

contentBlocks.original:
त्रि॒पादू॒र्ध्व उदै॒त्पुरु॑षः । पादो᳚-ऽस्ये॒हा-ऽऽभ॑वा॒त्पुनः॑ ।
ततो॒ विष्व॒ङ्​व्य॑क्रामत् । सा॒श॒ना॒न॒श॒ने अ॒भि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tri̠pādū̠rdhva udai̠tpuru̍ṣaḥ । pādō̎-'syē̠hā-''bha̍vā̠tpuna̍ḥ ।
tatō̠ viṣva̠ṅ​vya̍krāmat । sā̠śa̠nā̠na̠śa̠nē a̠bhi ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The three parts of the purusha is high above (in transcendental realm), and his one part becomes the creation again and again. There, in the creation, he pervades all the living ( who eats ) and the non-living ( who does not eat ) beings.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रिपाद् (tripād) – three parts (tri:three | pād:parts); ऊर्ध्व (ūrdhva) – above; उदैत् (udait) – arose; पूरुषः (pūruṣaḥ) – Purusha; पादः (pādaḥ) – one part; अस्य (asya) – his; इह (iha) – here; अभवत् (abhavat) – became; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; ततः (tataḥ) – then; विष्वङ् (viṣvaṅ) – everywhere; व्यक्रामत् (vyakrāmat) – pervaded; साशन (sāśana) – with food (sa:with | aśana:food); अनशने (anaśane) – without food (an:without | aśane:food); अभि (abhi) – towards;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मा᳚द्वि॒राड॑जायत । वि॒राजो॒ अधि॒ पूरु॑षः ।
स जा॒तो अत्य॑रिच्यत । प॒श्चाद्भूमि॒मथो॑ पु॒रः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmā̎dvi̠rāḍa̍jāyata । vi̠rājō̠ adhi̠ pūru̍ṣaḥ ।
sa jā̠tō atya̍richyata । pa̠śchādbhūmi̠mathō̍ pu̠raḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: From purusha was born the virat; (the virat came into being) from the presence of the shining purusha (who remained as the background or substratum of virat); he (i.e. the virat) created the earth, by manifesting her from his own being as substratum.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from that; विराट् (virāṭ) – Virat; अजायत (ajāyata) – was born; विराजः (virājaḥ) – Viraj; अधि (adhi) – above; पूरुषः (pūruṣaḥ) – Purusha; सः (saḥ) – he; जातः (jātaḥ) – born; अत्यरिच्यत (atyaricyata) – expanded; पश्चात् (paścāt) – afterwards; भूमिम् (bhūmim) – earth; अथ (atha) – then; पुरः (puraḥ) – forward;

contentBlocks.original:
यत्पुरु॑षेण ह॒विषा᳚ । दे॒वा य॒ज्ञमत॑न्वत ।
व॒स॒न्तो अ॑स्यासी॒दाज्यम्᳚ । ग्री॒ष्म इ॒ध्मश्श॒रध्ध॒विः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yatpuru̍ṣēṇa ha̠viṣā̎ । dē̠vā ya̠jñamata̍nvata ।
va̠sa̠ntō a̍syāsī̠dājyam̎ । grī̠ṣma i̠dhmaśśa̠radhdha̠viḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With the purusha as the (sacrificial) fire, the deva (the shining one, referring to virat) continued the yagna (sacrifice of creation), spring was (created as) the clarified butter (of that yagna), summer was (created as) the fuel (of that yagna), and autumn was (created as) the havis (sacrificial offering of that yagna).
contentBlocks.meanings: यत् (yat) – which; पुरुषेण (puruṣeṇa) – with the purusha; हविषा (haviṣā) – with the offering; देवाः (devāḥ) – the gods; यज्ञम् (yajñam) – sacrifice; अतन्वत (atanvata) – continued; वसन्तः (vasantaḥ) – spring; अस्य (asya) – of this; आसीत् (āsīt) – was; आज्यम् (ājyaṃ) – clarified butter; ग्रीष्मः (grīṣmaḥ) – summer; इध्मः (idhmaḥ) – fuel; शरद् (śarad) – autumn; धविः (dhaviḥ) – sacrificial offering;

contentBlocks.original:
स॒प्तास्या॑सन्परि॒धयः॑ । त्रि-स्स॒प्त स॒मिधः॑ कृ॒ताः ।
दे॒वा यद्य॒ज्ञ-न्त॑न्वा॒नाः । अब॑ध्न॒न्-पुरु॑ष-म्प॒शुम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa̠ptāsyā̍sanpari̠dhaya̍ḥ । tri-ssa̠pta sa̠midha̍ḥ kṛ̠tāḥ ।
dē̠vā yadya̠jña-nta̍nvā̠nāḥ । aba̍dhna̠n-puru̍ṣa-mpa̠śum ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By making seven enclosures with three times seven sacrificial firewood, the deva (the shining one referring to virat) in that yagna (sacrifice of creation), bound the infinite expanse of the purusha as (apparently) finite living beings (pashu).
contentBlocks.meanings: सप्त (sapta) – seven; अस्य (asya) – of this; आसन (āsan) – were; परिधयः (paridhayaḥ) – enclosures; त्रिः (triḥ) – three times; सप्त (sapta) – seven; समिधः (samidhaḥ) – sacrificial firewood; कृताः (kṛtāḥ) – made; देवाः (devāḥ) – devas; यत् (yat) – which; यज्ञं (yajñaṃ) – sacrifice; तन्वानाः (tanvānāḥ) – performing; अबध्नन् (abadhnan) – bound; पुरुषं (puruṣaṃ) – Purusha; पशुम् (paśum) – animal;

contentBlocks.original:
तं-यँ॒ज्ञ-म्ब॒र्॒हिषि॒ प्रौक्षन्॑ । पुरु॑ष-ञ्जा॒तम॑ग्र॒तः ।
तेन॑ दे॒वा अय॑जन्त । सा॒ध्या ऋष॑यश्च॒ ये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṃ ya̠jña-mba̠r̠hiṣi̠ praukṣan̍ । puru̍ṣa-ñjā̠tama̍gra̠taḥ ।
tēna̍ dē̠vā aya̍janta । sā̠dhyā ṛṣa̍yaścha̠ yē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The first divine men were created as the holy water sprinkled with the kusa grass in that yagna (sacrifice of creation). the first divine men were the sadhya devas and the rishis, who were created by him, the deva (the shining one, referring to virat), who performed the yagna. These rishis were not human but divine rishis like saptarshis created directly by virat.
contentBlocks.meanings: तं (taṃ) – that; यज्ञं (yajñaṃ) – sacrifice; बर्हिषि (barhiṣi) – on the sacrificial grass; प्रौक्षन् (praukṣan) – sprinkled; पुरुषं (puruṣaṃ) – man; जातम् (jātam) – born; अग्रतः (agrataḥ) – in the beginning; तेन (tena) – by him; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; अयजन्त (ayajanta) – worshipped; साध्याः (sādhyāḥ) – Sadhyas; ऋषयः (ṛṣayaḥ) – sages; च (ca) – and; ये (ye) – who;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मा᳚द्य॒ज्ञाथ्स॑र्व॒हुतः॑ । सम्भृ॑त-म्पृषदा॒ज्यम् ।
प॒शूग्-स्ताग्-श्च॑क्रे वाय॒व्यान्॑ । आ॒र॒ण्यान्-ग्रा॒म्याश्च॒ ये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmā̎dya̠jñāthsa̍rva̠huta̍ḥ । sambhṛ̍ta-mpṛṣadā̠jyam ।
pa̠śūg-stāg-ścha̍krē vāya̠vyān̍ । ā̠ra̠ṇyān-grā̠myāścha̠ yē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: From the complete offering of his (i.e. virat's) yagna (sacrifice of creation) was obtained ghee mixed with coagulated milk, which (i.e. the ghee and milk) are (the created) animals, both of air (birds) and of forests (wild animals) and villages (domestic animals).
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from that; यज्ञात् (yajñāt) – from the sacrifice; सर्वहुतः (sarvahutaḥ) – complete offering (sarva:all | hutaḥ:offering); सम्भृतं (sambhṛtaṃ) – obtained; पृषदाज्यम् (pṛṣadājyam) – ghee mixed with milk (pṛṣat:coagulated milk | ājyam:ghee); पशून् (paśūn) – animals; ताँश्च (tām̐śca) – and those; चक्रे (cakre) – created; वायव्यान् (vāyavyān) – of air (birds); आरण्यान् (āraṇyān) – of forests (wild animals); ग्राम्याः (grāmyāḥ) – of villages (domestic animals); च (ca) – and; ये (ye) – which;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मा᳚द्य॒ज्ञाथ्स॑र्व॒हुतः॑ । ऋच॒-स्सामा॑नि जज्ञिरे ।
छन्दाग्ं॑सि जज्ञिरे॒ तस्मा᳚त् । यजु॒स्तस्मा॑दजायत ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmā̎dya̠jñāthsa̍rva̠huta̍ḥ । ṛcha̠-ssāmā̍ni jajñirē ।
Chandāg̍ṃsi jajñirē̠ tasmā̎t । yaju̠stasmā̍dajāyata ॥
contentBlocks.translation: From the complete offering of his (i.e. virat's) yagna (sacrifice of creation) was born the rig veda and sama veda, the chandas (vedic meters) were born from him, and the yajur veda was born from him.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from that; यज्ञात् (yajñāt) – from the sacrifice; सर्वहुत (sarvahuta) – complete offering (sarva:all | huta:offering); ऋचः (ṛcaḥ) – Rig Veda hymns; सामानि (sāmāni) – Sama Veda hymns; जज्ञिरे (jajñire) – were born; छन्दांसि (chandāṃsi) – Vedic meters; जज्ञिरे (jajñire) – were born; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from that; यजुः (yajuḥ) – Yajur Veda; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from that; अजायत (ajāyata) – was born;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मा॒दश्वा॑ अजायन्त । ये के चो॑भ॒याद॑तः ।
गावो॑ ह जज्ञिरे॒ तस्मा᳚त् । तस्मा᳚ज्जा॒ता अ॑जा॒वयः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmā̠daśvā̍ ajāyanta । yē kē chō̍bha̠yāda̍taḥ ।
gāvō̍ ha jajñirē̠ tasmā̎t । tasmā̎jjā̠tā a̍jā̠vaya̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: From him (i.e. virat) was born the horses, and all those animals who has teeth in both jaws, from him (i.e. virat) was born the cows, and from him was born all types of goats.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from him; अश्वाः (aśvāḥ) – horses; अजायन्त (ajāyanta) – were born; ये (ye) – who; के (ke) – which; च (ca) – and; उभयादतः (ubhayādataḥ) – with teeth in both jaws (ubhayā:both | dataḥ:teeth); गावः (gāvaḥ) – cows; ह (ha) – indeed; जज्ञिरे (jajñire) – were born; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from him; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from him; जाताः (jātāḥ) – were born; अजावयः (ajāvayaḥ) – all types of goats (ajā:goats | vayaḥ:types);

contentBlocks.original:
यत्पुरु॑षं॒-व्यँ॑दधुः । क॒ति॒था व्य॑कल्पयन्न् ।
मुख॒-ङ्किम॑स्य॒ कौ बा॒हू । कावू॒रू पादा॑वुच्येते ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yatpuru̍ṣa̠ṃ vya̍dadhuḥ । ka̠ti̠thā vya̍kalpayann ।
mukha̠-ṅkima̍sya̠ kau bā̠hū । kāvū̠rū pādā̍vuchyētē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: What did the purusha (i.e. virat) hold within him? How many parts were assigned in his huge form? What was his mouth? What was his arms? What was his thighs? And what was his feet?
contentBlocks.meanings: यत् (yat) – what; पुरुषम् (puruṣam) – Purusha; व्यदधुः (vyadadhuḥ) – divided; कतिधा (katidhā) – into how many parts; विकल्पयन् (vikalpayān) – assigned; मुखम् (mukham) – mouth; किम् (kim) – what; अस्य (asya) – his; कौ (kau) – which; बाहू (bāhū) – arms; का (kā) – what; ऊरू (ūrū) – thighs; पादाः (pādāḥ) – feet; उच्येते (ucyete) – are called;

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रा॒ह्म॒णो᳚-ऽस्य॒ मुख॑मासीत् । बा॒हू रा॑ज॒न्यः॑ कृ॒तः ।
ऊ॒रू तद॑स्य॒ यद्वैश्यः॑ । प॒द्भ्याग्ं शू॒द्रो अ॑जायत ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brā̠hma̠ṇō̎-'sya̠ mukha̍māsīt । bā̠hū rā̍ja̠nya̍ḥ kṛ̠taḥ ।
ū̠rū tada̍sya̠ yadvaiśya̍ḥ । pa̠dbhyāgṃ śū̠drō a̍jāyata ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The Brahmanas were his mouth, the kshatriyas became his arms, the vaishyas were his thighs, and the shudras were assigned to his feet.
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्राह्मणः (brāhmaṇaḥ) – Brahmanas; अस्य (asya) – his; मुखम् (mukham) – mouth; आसीत् (āsīt) – was; बाहू (bāhū) – arms; राजन्यः (rājanyaḥ) – kshatriyas; कृतः (kṛtaḥ) – became; ऊरू (ūrū) – thighs; तत् (tat) – that; अस्य (asya) – his; यत् (yat) – which; वैश्यः (vaiśyaḥ) – vaishyas; पद्भ्याम् (padbhyām) – to the feet; शूद्रः (śūdraḥ) – shudras; अजायत (ajāyata) – were born;

contentBlocks.original:
च॒न्द्रमा॒ मन॑सो जा॒तः । चक्षो॒-स्सूर्यो॑ अजायत ।
मुखा॒दिन्द्र॑श्चा॒ग्निश्च॑ । प्रा॒णाद्वा॒युर॑जायत ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
cha̠ndramā̠ mana̍sō jā̠taḥ । chakṣō̠-ssūryō̍ ajāyata ।
mukhā̠dindra̍śchā̠gniścha̍ । prā̠ṇādvā̠yura̍jāyata ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The moon was born from his mind and the sun was born from his eyes, Indra and agni (fire) were born from his mouth, and vayu (wind) was born from his breath.
contentBlocks.meanings: चन्द्रमा (candramā) – moon; मनसः (manasaḥ) – from the mind; जातः (jātaḥ) – was born; चक्षोः (cakṣoḥ) – from the eyes; सूर्यः (sūryaḥ) – sun; अजायत (ajāyata) – was born; मुखात् (mukhāt) – from the mouth; इन्द्रः (indraḥ) – Indra; च (ca) – and; अग्निः (agniḥ) – fire; च (ca) – and; प्राणात् (prāṇāt) – from the breath; वायुः (vāyuḥ) – wind; अजायत (ajāyata) – was born;

contentBlocks.original:
नाभ्या॑ आसीद॒न्तरि॑क्षम् । शी॒र्​ष्णो द्यौ-स्सम॑वर्तत ।
प॒द्भ्या-म्भूमि॒र्दिश॒-श्श्रोत्रा᳚त् । तथा॑ लो॒काग्ं अ॑कल्पयन्न् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nābhyā̍ āsīda̠ntari̍kṣam । śī̠r​ṣṇō dyau-ssama̍vartata ।
pa̠dbhyā-mbhūmi̠rdiśa̠-śśrōtrā̎t । tathā̍ lō̠kāgṃ a̍kalpayann ॥
contentBlocks.translation: His navel became the antariksha (the intermediate space between heaven and earth), his head sustained the heaven, from his feet the earth (was sustained), and from his ears the directions (were sustained); in this manner all the worlds were regulated by him.
contentBlocks.meanings: नाभ्या (nābhyā) – from the navel; आसीत् (āsīt) – became; अन्तरिक्षं (antarikṣaṃ) – the intermediate space; शीर्ष्णः (śīrṣṇaḥ) – from the head; द्यौः (dyauḥ) – heaven; समवर्तत (samavartata) – sustained; पद्भ्याम् (padbhyām) – from the feet; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – the earth; दिशः (diśaḥ) – the directions; श्रोत्रात् (śrotrāt) – from the ears; तथा (tathā) – in this manner; लोकान् (lokān) – the worlds; अकल्पयन् (akalpayan) – regulated;

contentBlocks.original:
वेदा॒हमे॒त-म्पुरु॑ष-म्म॒हान्तम्᳚ । आ॒दि॒त्यव॑र्ण॒-न्तम॑स॒स्तु पा॒रे ।
सर्वा॑णि रू॒पाणि॑ वि॒चित्य॒ धीरः॑ । नामा॑नि कृ॒त्वा-ऽभि॒वद॒न्॒, यदा-ऽऽस्ते᳚ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdā̠hamē̠ta-mpuru̍ṣa-mma̠hāntam̎ । ā̠di̠tyava̍rṇa̠-ntama̍sa̠stu pā̠rē ।
sarvā̍ṇi rū̠pāṇi̍ vi̠chitya̠ dhīra̍ḥ । nāmā̍ni kṛ̠tvā-'bhi̠vada̠n̠, yadā-''stē̎ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I know this great Purusha, radiant as the sun, beyond all darkness. The wise one, having discerned all forms and having created their names, speaks of them — and thus He abides eternally.
contentBlocks.meanings: वेद-अहम् (vēda-aham) – I know (vēda:I know | aham:I); एतम्-पुरुषम्-महान्तम् (ētam-puruṣam-mahāntam) – this great Purusha (ētam:this | puruṣam:Purusha | mahāntam:great); आदित्य-वर्णम् (āditya-varṇam) – sun-colored, radiant as the sun (āditya:sun | varṇam:colored/hued); तमसः तु पारे (tamasaḥ tu pārē) – beyond darkness indeed (tamasaḥ:of darkness | tu:indeed | pārē:beyond); सर्वाणि रूपाणि (sarvāṇi rūpāṇi) – all forms (sarvāṇi:all | rūpāṇi:forms); विचित्य (vichitya) – having discerned/distinguished (vichitya:having discerned); धीरः (dhīraḥ) – the wise one (dhīraḥ:wise one); नामानि कृत्वा (nāmāni kṛtvā) – having made/created names (nāmāni:names | kṛtvā:having made); अभिवदन् (abhivadan) – speaking of, declaring (abhivadan:speaking/declaring); यदा आस्ते (yadā āstē) – when/as He abides (yadā:when | āstē:He abides/sits);

contentBlocks.original:
धा॒ता पु॒रस्ता॒द्यमु॑दाज॒हार॑ । श॒क्रः प्रवि॒द्वा-न्प्र॒दिश॒श्चत॑स्रः ।
तमे॒वं-विँ॒द्वान॒मृत॑ इ॒ह भ॑वति । नान्यः पन्था॒ अय॑नाय विद्यते ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhā̠tā pu̠rastā̠dyamu̍dāja̠hāra̍ । śa̠kraḥ pravi̠dvā-npra̠diśa̠śchata̍sraḥ ।
tamē̠vaṃ vi̠dvāna̠mṛta̍ i̠ha bha̍vati । nānyaḥ panthā̠ aya̍nāya vidyatē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhata, the Creator, brought forth this Purusha from the beginning. Indra, the mighty one, knowing him fully, proclaimed him to the four quarters of the universe. He who knows this Purusha thus becomes immortal even here in this world. There is no other path to liberation.
contentBlocks.meanings: धाता (dhātā) – the Creator, Dhata (dhātā:the Creator); पुरस्तात् (purastāt) – from the beginning, in front (purastāt:from the beginning); यम् उदाजहार (yam udājahāra) – whom he brought forth (yam:whom | udājahāra:brought forth); शक्रः (śakraḥ) – Indra, the mighty one (śakraḥ:Indra); प्रविद्वान् (pravidvān) – knowing fully (pravidvān:knowing fully); प्रदिशः चतस्रः (pradiśaḥ chatasraḥ) – the four quarters/directions (pradiśaḥ:quarters | chatasraḥ:four); तम् एवम् विद्वान् (tam ēvam vidvān) – he who knows him thus (tam:him | ēvam:thus | vidvān:knowing); अमृतः (amṛtaḥ) – immortal (amṛtaḥ:immortal); इह भवति (iha bhavati) – becomes here (iha:here | bhavati:becomes); न अन्यः पन्थाः (na anyaḥ panthāḥ) – no other path (na:not | anyaḥ:other | panthāḥ:path); अयनाय विद्यते (ayanāya vidyatē) – exists for attainment/liberation (ayanāya:for attainment | vidyatē:exists);

contentBlocks.original:
य॒ज्ञेन॑ य॒ज्ञम॑यजन्त दे॒वाः । तानि॒ धर्मा॑णि प्रथ॒मान्या॑सन्न् ।
ते ह॒ नाक॑-म्महि॒मानः॑ सचन्ते । यत्र॒ पूर्वे॑ सा॒ध्या-स्सन्ति॑ दे॒वाः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ya̠jñēna̍ ya̠jñama̍yajanta dē̠vāḥ । tāni̠ dharmā̍ṇi pratha̠mānyā̍sann ।
tē ha̠ nāka̍-mmahi̠māna̍-ssachantē । yatra̠ pūrvē̍ sā̠dhyā-ssanti̍ dē̠vāḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The devas performed the external yagna by meditating on the real yagna (i.e. contemplating on the purusha who is shining behind everything); and thus they first obtained the dharma (based on the oneness of the purusha), by meditating on the greatness of the chidakasha (blissful spiritual sky behind everyone, which is the essence of the purusha), during those earlier times, the spiritual aspirants became the shining one themselves.
contentBlocks.meanings: यज्ञेन (yajñena) – by sacrifice; यज्ञम् (yajñam) – sacrifice; अयजन्त (ayajanta) – performed; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; तानि (tāni) – those; धर्माणि (dharmāṇi) – righteous acts; प्रथमानि (prathamāni) – first; आसन् (āsan) – were; ते (te) – they; ह (ha) – indeed; नाकम् (nākam) – heaven; महिमानः (mahimānaḥ) – greatness; सचन्त (sacanta) – attain; यत्र (yatra) – where; पूर्वे (pūrve) – former; साध्याः (sādhyāḥ) – aspirants; सन्ति (santi) – are; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods;

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तरनारायणम्
अ॒द्भ्य-स्सम्भू॑तः पृथि॒व्यै रसा᳚च्च । वि॒श्वक॑र्मण॒-स्सम॑वर्त॒ताधि॑ ।
तस्य॒ त्वष्टा॑ वि॒दध॑द्रू॒पमे॑ति । तत्पुरु॑षस्य॒ विश्व॒माजा॑न॒मग्रे᳚ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttaranārāyaṇam
a̠dbhya-ssambhū̍taḥ pṛthi̠vyai rasā̎chcha । vi̠śvaka̍rmaṇa̠-ssama̍varta̠tādhi̍ ।
tasya̠ tvaṣṭā̍ vi̠dadha̍drū̠pamē̍ti । tatpuru̍ṣasya̠ viśva̠mājā̍na̠magrē̎ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: From the waters and from the essence of the earth he arose. From Vishwakarman, the cosmic architect, he manifested forth. Tvashta, the divine fashioner, shapes his form. Thus the entire universe of the Purusha came into being in the beginning.
contentBlocks.meanings: अद्भ्यः (adbhyaḥ) – from the waters (adbhyaḥ:from waters); सम्भूतः (sambhūtaḥ) – arose, was born (sambhūtaḥ:arose); पृथिव्यै (pṛthivyai) – from the earth (pṛthivyai:from earth); रसात् च (rasāt ca) – and from the essence (rasāt:from essence | ca:and); विश्वकर्मणः (viśvakarmaṇaḥ) – from Vishwakarman, the all-maker (viśva:all | karmaṇaḥ:from the maker); समवर्तत (samavartata) – he manifested, arose (samavartata:manifested); अधि (adhi) – forth, above (adhi:forth); तस्य (tasya) – of him, his (tasya:his); त्वष्टा (tvaṣṭā) – Tvashta, the divine fashioner (tvaṣṭā:Tvashta); विदधत् (vidadhat) – fashioning, shaping (vidadhat:fashioning); रूपम् (rūpam) – form (rūpam:form); एति (ēti) – brings forth, goes (ēti:brings forth); तत् (tat) – that (tat:that); पुरुषस्य (puruṣasya) – of the Purusha (puruṣasya:of Purusha); विश्वम् (viśvam) – the entire universe (viśvam:universe); आजानम् (ājānam) – birth, manifestation (ājānam:birth); अग्रे (agrē) – in the beginning (agrē:in the beginning);

contentBlocks.original:
वेदा॒हमे॒त-म्पुरु॑ष-म्म॒हान्तम्᳚ । आ॒दि॒त्यव॑र्ण॒-न्तम॑सः॒ पर॑स्तात् ।
तमे॒वं-विँ॒द्वान॒मृत॑ इ॒ह भ॑वति । नान्यः पन्था॑ विद्य॒ते-ऽय॑नाय ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdā̠hamē̠ta-mpuru̍ṣa-mma̠hāntam̎ । ā̠di̠tyava̍rṇa̠-ntama̍sa̠ḥ para̍stāt ।
tamē̠vaṃ vi̠dvāna̠mṛta̍ i̠ha bha̍vati । nānyaḥ panthā̍ vidya̠tē-'ya̍nāya ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I know this great Purusha, radiant as the sun, beyond all darkness. Only by knowing Him thus does one become immortal here in this very life. There is no other path to liberation.
contentBlocks.meanings: वेद-अहम् (vēda-aham) – I know (vēda:I know | aham:I); एतम्-पुरुषम् (ētam-puruṣam) – this Purusha (ētam:this | puruṣam:Purusha); महान्तम् (mahāntam) – the great one (mahāntam:great); आदित्य-वर्णम् (āditya-varṇam) – of sun-like radiance (āditya:sun | varṇam:color/radiance); तमसः परस्तात् (tamasaḥ parastāt) – beyond darkness (tamasaḥ:of darkness | parastāt:beyond); तम् एवम् (tam ēvam) – Him thus (tam:Him | ēvam:thus); विद्वान् (vidvān) – one who knows (vidvān:the knower); अमृतः (amṛtaḥ) – immortal (amṛtaḥ:immortal); इह भवति (iha bhavati) – becomes here (iha:here | bhavati:becomes); न अन्यः (na anyaḥ) – no other (na:not | anyaḥ:other); पन्थाः (panthāḥ) – path (panthāḥ:path); विद्यते (vidyatē) – exists (vidyatē:exists); अयनाय (ayanāya) – for attainment/liberation (ayanāya:for the goal/path);

contentBlocks.original:
प्र॒जाप॑तिश्चरति॒ गर्भे॑ अ॒न्तः । अ॒जाय॑मानो बहु॒धा विजा॑यते ।
तस्य॒ धीराः॒ परि॑जानन्ति॒ योनिम्᳚ । मरी॑चीना-म्प॒दमि॑च्छन्ति वे॒धसः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pra̠jāpa̍tiścharati̠ garbhē̍ a̠ntaḥ । a̠jāya̍mānō bahu̠dhā vijā̍yatē ।
tasya̠ dhīrā̠ḥ pari̍jānanti̠ yōnim̎ । marī̍chīnā-mpa̠dami̍chChanti vē̠dhasa̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Prajapati, the Lord of Creation, moves within the cosmic womb. Though unborn, He manifests in manifold forms. The wise ones truly comprehend His source and origin. The seers, the creators, seek to attain the realm of the Marichis — the radiant beings of light.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रजापतिः (prajāpatiḥ) – the Lord of Creation (prajā:progeny | patiḥ:lord); चरति (charati) – moves, dwells (charati:moves); गर्भे अन्तः (garbhē antaḥ) – within the womb (garbhē:in the womb | antaḥ:within); अजायमानः (ajāyamānaḥ) – though unborn (a:not | jāyamānaḥ:being born); बहुधा (bahudhā) – in many ways, manifold (bahudhā:in many ways); विजायते (vijāyatē) – is born, manifests (vijāyatē:is born forth); तस्य (tasya) – His, of Him (tasya:of Him); धीराः (dhīrāḥ) – the wise ones (dhīrāḥ:the wise); परिजानन्ति (parijānanti) – they fully know, comprehend (pari:fully | jānanti:they know); योनिम् (yōnim) – the source, origin, womb (yōnim:source); मरीचीनाम् (marīchīnām) – of the Marichis, radiant beings (marīchīnām:of the rays of light); पदम् (padam) – the abode, realm, station (padam:abode); इच्छन्ति (ichChanti) – they desire, seek (ichChanti:they desire); वेधसः (vēdhasaḥ) – the seers, creators, wise ones (vēdhasaḥ:the creators/seers);

contentBlocks.original:
यो दे॒वेभ्य॒ आत॑पति । यो दे॒वाना᳚-म्पु॒रोहि॑तः ।
पूर्वो॒ यो दे॒वेभ्यो॑ जा॒तः । नमो॑ रु॒चाय॒ ब्राह्म॑ये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yō dē̠vēbhya̠ āta̍pati । yō dē̠vānā̎-mpu̠rōhi̍taḥ ।
pūrvō̠ yō dē̠vēbhyō̍ jā̠taḥ । namō̍ ru̠chāya̠ brāhma̍yē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: He who blazes forth upon the gods, He who is the high priest of the gods, He who was born before the gods — salutations to that radiant Brahman.
contentBlocks.meanings: यो (yō) – He who (yō:He who); देवेभ्य (dēvēbhya) – upon the gods (dēvēbhya:upon the gods); आतपति (ātapati) – blazes forth, shines intensely (ātapati:blazes forth); देवानाम् (dēvānām) – of the gods (dēvānām:of the gods); पुरोहितः (purōhitaḥ) – the high priest (purōhitaḥ:high priest); पूर्वः (pūrvaḥ) – before, prior (pūrvaḥ:before); जातः (jātaḥ) – born (jātaḥ:born); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations (namaḥ:salutations); रुचाय (ruchāya) – to the radiant one (ruchāya:to the radiant one); ब्राह्मये (brāhmayē) – to the Brahman (brāhmayē:to the Brahman);

contentBlocks.original:
रुच॑-म्ब्रा॒ह्म-ञ्ज॒नय॑न्तः । दे॒वा अग्रे॒ तद॑ब्रुवन्न् ।
यस्त्वै॒व-म्ब्रा᳚ह्म॒णो वि॒द्यात् । तस्य॑ दे॒वा अस॒न् वशे᳚ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rucha̍-mbrā̠hma-ñja̠naya̍ntaḥ । dē̠vā agrē̠ tada̍bruvann ।
yastvai̠va-mbrā̎hma̠ṇō vi̠dyāt । tasya̍ dē̠vā asa̠n vaśē̎ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The gods, generating the luminous Brahman (the sacred Vedic hymns), spoke this truth in the beginning: Whosoever knows the Brahman in this manner — the gods shall be under his command.
contentBlocks.meanings: रुचम्-ब्राह्मम् (rucam-brāhmam) – the luminous Brahman, sacred hymn (rucam:brilliance, hymn | brāhmam:Brahman, sacred word); जनयन्तः (janayantaḥ) – generating, bringing forth (janayantaḥ:generating); देवाः (dēvāḥ) – the gods (dēvāḥ:gods); अग्रे (agrē) – in the beginning, at first (agrē:in the beginning); तत् अब्रुवन् (tat abruvann) – they spoke this (tat:this | abruvann:they spoke); यः तु (yaḥ tu) – whosoever indeed (yaḥ:who | tu:indeed); एवम् (ēvam) – in this manner, thus (ēvam:thus); ब्राह्मणः (brāhmaṇaḥ) – the Brahman, knower of Brahman (brāhmaṇaḥ:Brahman, sacred knowledge); विद्यात् (vidyāt) – should know, would know (vidyāt:would know); तस्य (tasya) – of him, his (tasya:of him); देवाः (dēvāḥ) – the gods (dēvāḥ:gods); असन् वशे (asan vaśē) – are under control (asan:are | vaśē:under control, in power);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीश्च॑ ते ल॒क्ष्मीश्च॒ पत्न्यौ᳚ । अ॒हो॒रा॒त्रे पा॒र्​श्वे ।
नक्ष॑त्राणि रू॒पम् । अ॒श्विनौ॒ व्यात्तम्᳚ ।
इ॒ष्ट-म्म॑निषाण । अ॒मु-म्म॑निषाण । सर्व॑-म्मनिषाण ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīścha̍ tē la̠kṣmīścha̠ patnyau̎ । a̠hō̠rā̠trē pā̠r​śvē ।
nakṣa̍trāṇi rū̠pam । a̠śvinau̠ vyāttam̎ ।
i̠ṣṭa-mma̍niṣāṇa । a̠mu-mma̍niṣāṇa । sarva̍-mmaniṣāṇa ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Modesty and Prosperity are Your consorts. Day and night are Your two sides. The stars are Your form. The Ashvins are Your open mouth. Grant me what I desire. Grant me that which I seek. Grant me everything.
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रीः-च (hrīḥ-ca) – and modesty (hrīḥ:modesty | ca:and); ते (tē) – Your (tē:Your); लक्ष्मीः-च (lakṣmīḥ-ca) – and Lakshmi/prosperity (lakṣmīḥ:Lakshmi, prosperity | ca:and); पत्न्यौ (patnyau) – the two consorts (patnyau:two wives, consorts); अहोरात्रे (ahōrātrē) – day and night (ahaḥ:day | rātrē:night); पार्श्वे (pārśvē) – the two sides (pārśvē:two sides, flanks); नक्षत्राणि (nakṣatrāṇi) – the stars, constellations (nakṣatrāṇi:stars, lunar mansions); रूपम् (rūpam) – form, appearance (rūpam:form); अश्विनौ (aśvinau) – the two Ashvins (aśvinau:the twin Ashvin gods); व्यात्तम् (vyāttam) – open mouth (vyāttam:opened wide, gaping mouth); इष्टम्-मनिषाण (iṣṭam-maniṣāṇa) – grant what is desired (iṣṭam:desired thing | maniṣāṇa:grant, bestow); अमुम्-मनिषाण (amum-maniṣāṇa) – grant that (amum:that | maniṣāṇa:grant, bestow); सर्वम्-मनिषाण (sarvam-maniṣāṇa) – grant everything (sarvam:all, everything | maniṣāṇa:grant, bestow);

contentBlocks.original:
शान्ति मन्त्रः
तच्छं॒-योँरावृ॑णीमहे । गा॒तुं-यँ॒ज्ञाय॑ । गा॒तुं-यँ॒ज्ञप॑तये । दैवी᳚ स्व॒स्तिर॑स्तु नः । स्व॒स्तिर्मानु॑षेभ्यः । ऊ॒र्ध्व-ञ्जि॑गातु भेष॒जम् । श-न्नो॑ अस्तु द्वि॒पदे᳚ । श-ञ्चतु॑ष्पदे ।
ॐ शान्ति॒-श्शान्ति॒-श्शान्तिः॑ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
Shanti Mantra
tachCha̠ṃ yōrāvṛ̍ṇīmahē । gā̠tuṃ ya̠jñāya̍ । gā̠tuṃ ya̠jñapa̍tayē । daivī̎ sva̠stira̍stu naḥ । sva̠stirmānu̍ṣēbhyaḥ । ū̠rdhva-ñji̍gātu bhēṣa̠jam । śa-nnō̍ astu dvi̠padē̎ । śa-ñchatu̍ṣpadē ।
ōṃ śānti̠-śśānti̠-śśānti̍ḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: We invoke that auspicious blessing of the Divine. May there be a clear path for the sacrifice. May there be a clear path for the lord of the sacrifice. May divine grace be upon us. May well-being attend all human beings. May the healing remedy rise upward and prevail. May there be peace for all two-footed beings. May there be peace for all four-footed creatures. Om, peace, peace, peace.
contentBlocks.meanings: तत्-शम् (tat-śam) – that auspiciousness (tat:that | śam:auspiciousness, peace); योः आवृणीमहे (yōḥ āvṛṇīmahē) – we choose, we invoke (yōḥ:of which | āvṛṇīmahē:we choose, we invoke); गातुम् (gātum) – path, way, progress (gātum:path, way); यज्ञाय (yajñāya) – for the sacrifice (yajñāya:for the sacrifice); यज्ञ-पतये (yajña-patayē) – for the lord of sacrifice (yajña:sacrifice | patayē:for the lord); दैवी (daivī) – divine (daivī:divine); स्वस्तिः (svastiḥ) – well-being, auspiciousness (svastiḥ:well-being); अस्तु नः (astu naḥ) – may it be for us (astu:may it be | naḥ:for us); मानुषेभ्यः (mānuṣēbhyaḥ) – for human beings (mānuṣēbhyaḥ:for humans); ऊर्ध्वम् (ūrdhvam) – upward (ūrdhvam:upward); जिगातु (jigātu) – may it go, may it prevail (jigātu:may it go); भेषजम् (bhēṣajam) – remedy, medicine, healing (bhēṣajam:medicine, healing); शम् नः (śam naḥ) – peace for us (śam:peace | naḥ:for us); द्विपदे (dvipadē) – for two-footed beings (dvi:two | padē:footed); चतुष्पदे (chatuṣpadē) – for four-footed beings (chatuḥ:four | padē:footed); ॐ (ōṃ) – the sacred syllable Om (ōṃ:Om); शान्तिः (śāntiḥ) – peace (śāntiḥ:peace);
